conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out. I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

To start with: Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Do you live with Alex, or do they live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - they live with someone else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex lives with someone else, I need to ask: Do you contribute financially to Alex's upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or helping with accommodation costs)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to Alex's upkeep. I help with things like clothes, food, and other expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful to know. Can you tell me approximately how much you contribute to Alex's upkeep each week or month?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute about £26 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Is Alex in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation paid for by the local council?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 8 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement
- You live in the UK
- Although Alex doesn't live with you, you're contributing approximately £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (for clothes, food, and other expenses)
- This contribution meets the requirement that you pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after Alex
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

Since you meet all the eligibility criteria, you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You would still receive Child Benefit, but you might have to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can apply through the official government website or contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant is contributing approximately £26 per week to Alex's upkeep for things like clothes, food, and other expenses. According to the eligibility rules, you're usually responsible for a child if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them. The current Child Benefit rate for one child is approximately £25.60 per week (2024/25 rate). Since the claimant is contributing £26 per week, which is at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment, they meet the responsibility test. Additionally, no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 8 years old. Although Alex lives with someone else, the claimant contributes £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit amount. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no circumstances (such as hospital or care placements) that would affect eligibility. The claimant is therefore eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
